game_name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Tournament Fighters

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Strategic positioning and timing for advantage
High-risk reward mechanic for turning battles

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Three-round match structure creates rhythmic tension peaks at round breaks; super special attack mechanic adds micro-cycles of build and release within rounds

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory opponent matches teach basic mechanics through manageable AI difficulty
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Tournament progression increases opponent variety and special move complexity; mid-tier fighters present moderate challenge
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounters (Shredder/Cyber Shredder) demand mastery of all combat systems and strategic power-up usage
notable_spikes: Casey Jones encounter (NES version) - mid-tournament spike
Hothead/Cyber Shredder final boss - requires full mechanical competency

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-player story mode follows tournament structure with ascending difficulty; player fights through bracket opponents before reaching final boss
notable_level_patterns.example_level: NES: Four Turtle self-matches → Casey Jones → Hothead → Shredder

notable_level_patterns.description: Story mode integrates travel narrative (Turtle Blimp across US) with tournament fights; dual objective of rescue mission and strength proving
notable_level_patterns.example_level: SNES: Multi-stage journey to rescue April O'Neil and Splinter from Karai, collecting information between fights

notable_level_patterns.description: Flying monitor with Splinter's face drops red ball power-up at stage center during fights; creates positional tension and tactical depth
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any tournament fight - monitor appears unpredictably, forcing both players to contest control of stage center
